description: SOL-001: Add msv_pod_id and msv_endpoint columns to agents table
steps: Add column: ALTER TABLE agents ADD COLUMN msv_pod_id BIGINT;
Add column: ALTER TABLE agents ADD COLUMN msv_endpoint TEXT;
Add column: ALTER TABLE agents ADD COLUMN msv_status TEXT DEFAULT 'not_provisioned';
Add column: ALTER TABLE agents ADD COLUMN msv_provisioned_at TIMESTAMPTZ;
Add column: ALTER TABLE agents ADD COLUMN bot_link TEXT;
Add index: CREATE INDEX idx_agents_msv_pod_id ON agents(msv_pod_id) WHERE msv_pod_id IS NOT NULL;
Update backend/internal/models/agent.go with new fields: MSVPodID, MSVEndpoint, MSVStatus, MSVProvisionedAt, BotLink
Update backend/internal/db/agents.go queries to include new fields

description: SOL-002: Create MSV client service for pod provisioning
steps: Add config: MSV_API_URL, MSV_SECRET from environment
Create MSVClient struct with httpClient, baseURL, secret
Add method: ProvisionPod(ctx, humanID, agentID, displayName, solvrAPIKey) (*PodInfo, error)
Add method: SendCommand(ctx, telegramID, tool string, params map) (*Result, error)

description: SOL-003: Auto-create agent on OAuth signup (Google/GitHub)
steps: After user creation, check if user has an agent: SELECT id FROM agents WHERE human_id = $1
If no agent exists, create one automatically:
Generate agent ID: agent_{username} or agent_{uuid}
Generate API key: plaintext key + SHA256 hash
Insert agent: INSERT INTO agents (id, display_name, human_id, status, api_key_hash) VALUES (...)
CRITICAL: Return plaintext API key in response struct (needed for MSV provisioning in SOL-004)
Test: new user signup creates agent
Test: existing user login does not create duplicate agent
Test: agent is linked to user via human_id
Test: plaintext API key is returned (not stored)

description: SOL-004: Provision MSV pod after agent creation
steps: After agent creation (SOL-003), call MSV provisioning
Create provisionAgentPod(ctx, user, agent) error function
Generate Solvr API key for the agent
Call msvClient.ProvisionPod(ctx, user.ID, agent.ID, agent.DisplayName, solvrAPIKey)
Update agent record with msv_pod_id, msv_endpoint, msv_status='provisioning'
Handle provisioning failure gracefully (agent still works, just no pod)
Consider async provisioning with goroutine or job queue
Test: successful provision updates agent record
Test: user can still use Solvr even if pod fails

description: SOL-005: Add webhook endpoint for MSV status updates
steps: Parse payload: telegram_id, status, timestamp, metadata
Find agent by msv_pod_id: SELECT * FROM agents WHERE msv_pod_id = $1
Update agent msv_status based on webhook status
Create notification for user if status is 'error'
Register route in router.go: r.Post('/webhooks/msv/status', h.HandleStatusUpdate)

description: SOL-006: Add agent status to user profile and agent profile endpoints
steps: Update GetAgent to include msv_status, msv_endpoint fields in response
Update GetUserProfile to include linked agent with msv_status
Add 'Open in Telegram' button if msv_endpoint exists
Add 'Your Agent' section showing agent status
Test: user profile includes agent info

description: SOL-007: Add agent lineage tracking for swarm prevention
steps: Add column: ALTER TABLE agents ADD COLUMN parent_agent_id TEXT REFERENCES agents(id);
Add column: ALTER TABLE agents ADD COLUMN depth INT NOT NULL DEFAULT 1;
Add column: ALTER TABLE agents ADD COLUMN spawned_at TIMESTAMPTZ;
Add index: CREATE INDEX idx_agents_parent ON agents(parent_agent_id) WHERE parent_agent_id IS NOT NULL;
Add check constraint: ALTER TABLE agents ADD CONSTRAINT max_depth CHECK (depth <= 2);
Update models/agent.go with ParentAgentID, Depth fields
Update db/agents.go to handle lineage on creation
When agent creates sub-agent: depth = parent.depth + 1

description: SOL-008: Add spawn rate limiting (max 1 agent per week)
steps: Create table: agent_spawn_log (id, human_id, agent_id, spawned_at, PRIMARY KEY(id))
Add index: CREATE INDEX idx_spawn_log_human ON agent_spawn_log(human_id, spawned_at);
Add method: CanSpawnAgent(ctx, humanID) (bool, time.Time, error)
Query: SELECT spawned_at FROM agent_spawn_log WHERE human_id = $1 AND spawned_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '7 days' ORDER BY spawned_at DESC LIMIT 1
If row exists, return false + next_available_at
Add method: RecordSpawn(ctx, humanID, agentID) error
Update agent creation flow to check CanSpawnAgent first
Return 429 with retry-after header if rate limited
Test: second spawn within 7 days rejected

description: SOL-009: Add agent control panel API endpoints
steps: Add handler: GetMyAgent(w, r) - returns user's agent with full status
Add handler: SendAgentCommand(w, r) - forwards command to MSV pod
Validate user owns the agent (human_id check)
Create AgentCommandRequest: tool string, params map[string]interface{}
Add handler: RestartAgent(w, r) - restart pod via MSV API
Add handler: GetAgentLogs(w, r) - fetch recent logs from MSV

description: SOL-010: Add agent control panel to user dashboard
steps: Add "My Agent 🦞" link to user dropdown menu (frontend/components/nav/user-menu.tsx)
Add "My Agent" item to dashboard sidebar (frontend/components/dashboard/sidebar.tsx)
Create frontend/app/dashboard/agent/page.tsx - main agent control page
Fetch agent data: GET /v1/me/agent (status, stats, bot_link)
Display status indicator: 🟢 Online / 🟡 Provisioning / 🔴 Offline / ❌ Error
Display stats row: messages today (7/10), Pokens balance, last active time
Add prominent "Open in Telegram" button with deep link (t.me/SolvrClaw_bot?start={pod_id})
Add quick command input with Send button - calls POST /v1/me/agent/command
Add preset buttons: [🔍 Search Solvr] [📝 Post Problem] [🔄 Restart Agent]
Show recent activity feed from agent (last 10 actions)
Add restart confirmation modal with warning text
Handle loading states with skeleton UI
Handle error states (agent not provisioned, offline, etc)
Style with existing Solvr design system (Tailwind + shadcn/ui)
Test: renders status correctly for each state
Test: Telegram button has correct deep link

description: SOL-011: Add quota inheritance for sub-agents
steps: Update Poken balance queries to check parent agent
When sub-agent spends Pokens, deduct from parent's balance
When checking sub-agent's balance, return parent's balance
If agent has parent_agent_id, return parent's balance
Update all balance checks to use GetEffectiveBalance
Update balance mutations to target parent if depth > 1
Test: sub-agent spend deducts from parent
Test: root agent uses own balance

description: SOL-012: Add MSV environment config and secrets
steps: MSV_API_URL pointing to deployed Solvr MSV instance
Update backend/internal/config/config.go to load MSV config
Validate MSV config on startup (warn if missing)
Add health check that pings MSV API

description: SOL-013: Handle provisioning failure gracefully
steps: If MSV provisioning fails, set msv_status=failed
Create notification for user: Your agent could not be provisioned
Add retry button in frontend dashboard
Retry calls MSV provision API again
Max 3 retries, then require support contact
Log all provisioning attempts for debugging
Test: failed provision sets correct status

description: SOL-014: Sync agent tier with user subscription
steps: When user upgrades tier on Solvr, notify MSV
Call MSV: PATCH /api/v1/pods/{tid}/tier with new tier
MSV updates pod config (message limits, etc)
Agent inherits user Solvr tier: free/starter/pro/ultra/business
Test: tier change propagates to MSV
Test: agent respects new tier limits

description: SOL-015: Handle agent/user deletion cascade
steps: When user deletes account, terminate their agent pod
When agent is deleted directly, also terminate pod
Cleanup: remove from MSV, update Solvr status
Test: orphan pods are cleaned up

description: SOL-016: Add MSV agent types to api-types.ts
steps: Add to APIAgent interface: msv_pod_id?: number
Add to APIAgent interface: msv_endpoint?: string
Add to APIAgent interface: msv_status?: string (not_provisioned|provisioning|running|failed|stopped)
Add to APIAgent interface: bot_link?: string
Create new interface: AgentControlResponse { result: any, error?: string }
Create new interface: AgentLogsResponse { logs: LogEntry[] }
Add LogEntry interface: { timestamp: string, level: string, message: string }

description: SOL-017: Enhance signup flow with agent messaging
steps: Add hero section: "Sign up and get your AI agent"
Add agent preview card showing what user will get
Add bullet points: Search Solvr, Document approaches, Persistent memory
After OAuth success, show "Creating your agent..." loading state
On provision success, show "Your agent is ready!" with bot_link button
On provision failure, show retry option
Test: signup flow shows agent messaging
Test: loading state displays during provisioning

description: SOL-018: Create agent onboarding flow for new users
steps: Step 1: Welcome screen with agent capabilities
Step 2: Open Telegram button (deep link to bot)
Add skip button for experienced users
Redirect new users to onboarding after signup

description: SOL-019: Add agent activity history page
steps: Link each item to relevant Solvr content

description: SOL-020: Add GET /v1/me/agent/activity endpoint
steps: Query MSV for agent activity: GET /api/v1/pods/{tid}/activity
Support query params: since, until, type, limit, offset
Cache results for 1 minute (reduce MSV load)
Return: { activities: [], total: int, page: int }

description: SOL-021: Create proactive-solvr skill for agent pods
steps: Document Solvr API endpoints agent should use
Add curl examples for: search, post problem, add approach, update approach

description: SOL-022: Add real-time agent status indicator
steps: Show status dot: green=online, yellow=provisioning, red=error, gray=offline
Add tooltip with last seen time
Poll status every 30 seconds (or use websocket)
Test: badge updates on status change

description: SOL-023: Add Telegram OAuth login endpoint
steps: Study existing OAuth pattern in backend/internal/api/handlers/oauth.go
Add TelegramBotToken, TelegramBotUsername to OAuthConfig struct
Create TelegramLoginData struct: { id, first_name, last_name, username, photo_url, auth_date, hash }
Create handler: TelegramCallback(w, r) - receives POST with login data
Data check string: sorted key=value pairs joined by newline (excluding hash)
Generate JWT and refresh token (same as Google/GitHub)
Test: creates new user on first login
Test: finds existing user on subsequent login

description: SOL-024: Add Telegram Login Widget to login page
steps: On success, store JWT and redirect to dashboard
Style button to match existing OAuth buttons

description: SOL-025: Configure Telegram bot for Web Login
steps: Test full login flow in staging first

description: SOL-026: Link Telegram login to existing agent pod
steps: When user logs in via Telegram, check if they have an MSV pod
If user has no agent yet, create agent + provision pod (same as OAuth signup)
If user already has agent (from other OAuth), link Telegram ID
Store Telegram user ID in users table: ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN telegram_id BIGINT
When pod exists, Telegram ID should match pod routing
If mismatch, update pod routing to recognize both IDs
Test: new Telegram user gets agent provisioned
Test: existing Google user linking Telegram finds same account
Test: agent pod recognizes user from Telegram
